sizing_rule.note: Position Size = Account Size × optimal_f / worst_case_loss. Example optimal_f = 0.25 produces TWR of 10.55; deviating ±15% (to 0.1 or 0.4) reduces TWR to 4.66. Calculate optimal_f from historical trade data; apply as fraction of capital based on perceived worst-case loss on next trade.

source_rule_text: Position size should be based on optimal f value, as deviating by 15% from optimal f (e.g., using .1 or .4 instead of optimal .25 f) significantly reduces terminal wealth relative (TWR) from 10.55 to 4.66.
